author: michellegdyason
description: A plugin for deep self-discovery journaling using AI. Use analysis history, and export options. Requires an OpenAI API key. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Michelle G Dyason
manifest.description: Deep self-discovery journaling using OpenAI or a local Ollama model, with analysis history and export options.
manifest.name: The Deleometer

name: The Deleometer

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Stable release for Obsidian scorecard compliance and network cleanup.

Changes in 1.0.5:
- removes the bundled OpenAI SDK from the runtime bundle
- uses explicit direct requests for OpenAI chat completions and the configured local Ollama endpoint only
- hardens API-key and error redaction so secrets do not leak into notices or logs
- keeps OpenAI mode available on mobile while clearly marking local runtime features as desktop-only
- passes npm run lint and npm run build with aligned release metadata

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Stable release for the analysis parsing fix.

Changes in 1.0.4:
- hardens multi-perspective analysis response parsing for broader AI response shapes
- recovers analyses when providers return nested objects or labels like perspective_key / perspectiveKey
- prevents journal analysis from failing with 'Analysis response did not include any usable perspectives' when the content is present but differently structured

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Stable release for Obsidian community plugin resubmission.

Changes from 1.0.2:
- rewrites the manifest description to avoid self-referential plugin wording
- keeps minAppVersion at 1.7.2 to match current API usage
- includes the current release assets for portal validation

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Stable release for Obsidian community plugin resubmission.

Highlights:
- merges the current beta line into main
- adds Myers-Briggs analysis as an optional perspective
- updates release metadata and README origin story
- raises minAppVersion to 1.7.2 to match current API usage
- includes the latest main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css assets

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Prevents local-model prose responses from failing the whole analysis when JSON was requested. Single-perspective prose is now preserved as the analysis text; full-analysis synthesis prose is saved as philosophy re-accumulation; further-reading and goal JSON failures degrade to empty optional outputs instead of aborting the completed run.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.44

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Makes analysis parsing more tolerant for local models by recovering perspective responses from exact keys, perspective titles, arrays of perspective objects, and object values with analysis/content/text fields. This addresses failures where local model responses were parsed but no usable perspective keys were found.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.43

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Adds an official Ollama download link in local AI settings and shows an estimated AI cost panel based on the number of selected analysis lenses. OpenAI mode estimates approximate gpt-4o-mini API spend; local Ollama mode shows zero API cost with local hardware/download caveats.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.42

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Improves local Ollama setup by detecting installed local models, offering an installed-model dropdown, explaining that Ollama must be installed and running, and adding a settings control to download a chosen Ollama model into the local library.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.41

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Adds an AI provider setting so users can choose OpenAI or a local Ollama model. Local mode sends journal and chat context only to the configured local endpoint, never falls back to OpenAI automatically, and can check the local Ollama model library for changes.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.40

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Reduces chronological batch size automatically when many lenses are selected, and adds more defensive JSON repair for malformed model responses. This should reduce whole-batch failures like unterminated strings and missing commas in analysis output.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.39

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Analysis no longer pre-generates suggested goals; draft goals are generated only when requested from the analysis popup. The popup buttons now show clearer pressed and busy states so clicks are visible while actions run.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.38

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Journal analyses now run as tracked background jobs so multiple saved journal entries can be analyzed in parallel safely. Duplicate analysis of the same note is blocked, and shared author-memory updates are serialized to avoid settings-write races.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.37

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Deleting a Deleometer goal note now immediately removes its linked milestone notes and Deleometer-owned calendar entries instead of leaving them behind for manual cleanup.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.36

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Moves Deleometer calendar sync to a dedicated Deleometer/Calendar folder, treats the top-level Deleometer folder as too broad for calendar event notes, and consolidates old Deleometer-owned calendar event duplicates during sync.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.35

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT testing. Adds a bipolar slider for the typology assessment, rejects absolute macOS paths in the Deleometer calendar folder setting, and clarifies that the calendar folder must be vault-relative.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.34

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
## BRAT beta

- add a clearer reflective assessment structure with a dedicated assessment hub
- keep the existing big five profile but label its theory explicitly as the five-factor model
- add separate reflective profiles for typology (myers-briggs-style) and maslow needs
- include the saved reflective profiles in AI context only when profile sharing is enabled
- clarify the mood trends dashboard with explicit per-entry values and scale guidance
- fix journal note date handling so note filenames and the frontmatter date use the user local date

Install with BRAT using version: 1.0.2-beta.33
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.33

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
## BRAT beta

- fix inspirational song generation so malformed or partial AI payloads no longer drop the song silently
- add a resilient local fallback song sketch when the AI payload is empty or incomplete
- keep warnings visible in Analysis Notes when the fallback path is used

Install with BRAT using version: 1.0.2-beta.32
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.32

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
- added major taxonomy expansion across philosophy, religion, media, clinical, decolonial, ecological, and strategy lenses
- added Dictionary mode setting with engage/copy options for dictionary-informed frames
- Druidic interpretation now ends with a short bardic poem or speech
- improved lineage/tradition metadata for several narrative, frame, feminist, clinical, and ecological perspectives
- updated README catalogue and total analysis count to 261
- validation: npm run build, npm run lint
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.31

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
- added Frankfurt School Critical Theory and Discourse Ethics
- added Moral Philosophy, Analytic Moral Philosophy, Continental Moral Philosophy, Feminist Ethics, Continental Feminist Philosophy, Enactivism, Continental Political Aesthetics, Hannah Arendt, Edith Cowan, and Diane "Ding" Dyason lenses
- made the analysis time estimate more visible by merging it into the main analysis notice
- updated README catalogue and total analysis count
- validation: npm run build, npm run lint
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.30

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
This beta adds the Set Theory lens and includes the new inspirational song stage.

Included in this build:
- added Set Theory to the philosophy chronology, aliases, metadata, and README catalogue
- added inspirational song generation after philosophy re-accumulation
- generates lyrics, hook, song rationale, and a locally rendered WAV sketch saved into the vault
- added settings for enabling song generation and choosing the songs folder

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.28

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
This beta packages the unpublished analysis updates into one BRAT release.

Included in this build:
- backfilled further reading suggestions across perspectives
- improved Julia Kristeva and Luce Irigaray framing to include both philosophy and psychoanalysis
- added Pierre Bourdieu and Jean Baudrillard lenses
- updated README analysis catalogue count and listings

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.27

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: BRAT beta enforcing 'learnt' instead of past-tense 'learned' in English AI output.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.26

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: BRAT beta adding chat from group syntheses plus Steiner Education and Crip Studies lenses.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.25

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: BRAT beta adding Ancient Egyptian Interpretation to the religious chronology and catalogue.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.24

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: BRAT beta with chronology adjustments for sociology, new vitalism and organicism lenses, source-lens-aware chat export/save behavior, per-goal milestone folders, source-note goal/milestone links, and generated notes opening in a right split.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.23

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: BRAT beta with the new Aesthetics lens, output language selection for English/French/German across analysis and chat, and the current README/beta feedback updates.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.22

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for milestone folders and milestone consolidation. Adds a default Deleometer/Milestones folder, commands and settings/dashboard controls to sync milestones into Markdown notes, and a consolidation workflow for similar milestone notes. Also updates the README and BRAT assets.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.21

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT. Adds repaired JSON parsing for AI responses so malformed responses with code fences, trailing commas, or lightly malformed object keys are recovered before a chronological batch is marked failed. This targets errors like expected double quoted property name in JSON during analysis batches.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.20

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT. Adds security and privacy controls: sensitive-detail redaction before AI calls, author memory and personality prompt-sharing toggles, full-journal chat sharing off by default, clear-key and clear-memory controls, stricter Deleometer link validation, README security/privacy guidance, and an esbuild dev dependency update that leaves npm audit clean.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.19

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT. Hides the OpenAI API key in settings by using a password input, disables autocomplete/spellcheck on the key field, documents that the key is still stored locally in Obsidian plugin data, and rebuilds plugin runtime.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.18

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds Australian Indigenous Philosophy Accumulated as the first chronological lens, plus Tessa Laird's Cinemal, Australian Legal Discourse, Mechanical Engineering Analysis, Ecology, Social-Ecological Systems Theory, Autoethnography, Grounded Theory, and Asian/Japanese, African/Zimbabwean, and Western parental guidance lenses. Updates chronology, grouped catalogue, prompt guidance, and migrations for users with all prior analyses enabled.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.9

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT. Adds Cartographic Analysis, Geography, Sociology, and Social Research Methods; places them in the strict chronological sequence; adds a Social, Spatial, and Research Methods settings group; updates README catalogue count to 135; and rebuilds plugin runtime.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.17

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT. Enforces strict chronological ordering for generated analyses, keeps groups as secondary lineage markers for settings and synthesis, updates README language, and rebuilds plugin runtime.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.16

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds Shaun Gallagher's Pattern Theory of Self lens after Gallagher's Embodied Self. Updates the README catalogue to 131 analyses, adds aliases for A Pattern Theory of Self, and adds prompt guidance for embodied, experiential, affective, intersubjective, narrative, extended, ecological, and normative dimensions of selfhood.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.15

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds Gregory Bateson's Ecology of Mind lens to Systems, Ecology, and Food Futures. Updates the README catalogue to 130 analyses and adds prompt guidance for communication, feedback loops, learning levels, double binds, relational patterns, and ecology of mind.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.14

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs, Montessori Method, Jean Piaget's Developmental Theory, Lev Vygotsky's Sociocultural Theory, and Organisational Transformation. Updates the README catalogue to 129 analyses, adds prompt guidance for developmental and organisational transformation frames, includes a Marlow/Maslow alias, and migrates full-analysis users to include the new lenses.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.13

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds Julia Kristeva's Abjection and Semiotic lens to Psychoanalytic and Clinical Psychologies, updates the README catalogue to 124 analyses, adds aliases and prompt guidance for Kristeva, and migrates full-analysis users to include the new lens.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.12

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds 11 lenses across the chronology: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz's Monadology, Moral Naturalism, Emmanuel Levinas' Ethics of the Other, Jürgen Habermas' Communicative Subject, Jean-Luc Nancy's Being-With, Music Songwriting Analysis, Marxian Analysis, Georges Bataillean Analysis, Frantz Fanonian Analysis, Quantum Theory Analysis, and Karen Barad's Agential Realism. Updates the README catalogue to 123 analyses, adds aliases for spelling variants, adds prompt guidance for these frames, and migrates full-analysis users to include the new lenses.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.11

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds a self and subjectivity expansion inside Philosophy as First Discipline, including René Descartes, John Locke, David Hume, Immanuel Kant, G.W.F. Hegel, Martin Heidegger, Jean-Paul Sartre, Simone de Beauvoir, Maurice Merleau-Ponty, P. F. Strawson, Harry Frankfurt, Sydney Shoemaker, Bernard Williams, Paul Ricoeur, Derek Parfit, Charles Taylor, Alasdair MacIntyre, Judith Butler, Catriona Mackenzie, Christine Korsgaard, Marya Schechtman, Linda Martín Alcoff, Kwame Anthony Appiah, Adriana Cavarero, Dan Zahavi, Shaun Gallagher, and Rosi Braidotti. Updates the chronological README catalogue to 112 analyses and migrates full-analysis users to include the new lenses.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.10

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description: Beta update for BRAT: adds Linguistic Analysis and Semiotic Analysis, reorders analysis groups and lenses into a rough historical chronology, preserves chronology during analysis generation even for existing saved settings, and updates the README catalogue to 74 analyses.
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.8

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Beta update for BRAT testing.

Changes:
- Adds a new Religious, Mythic, and Pagan Interpretations group.
- Adds Ancient Religious, Greek Gods, Roman Gods, Pagan, Druidic, First Testament / Hebrew, Second Testament / Christian, and Muslim interpretation lenses.
- Updates analysis prompting so religious and mythic frames are comparative, respectful, non-proselytizing, and interpretive rather than treated as universal truth claims.
- Updates README analysis catalogue count to 72 and lists the new group.

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
- git diff --check
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.7

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Beta update for BRAT testing.

Changes:
- Adds Cynics' Philosophy analysis lens.
- Improves goal suggestions by requiring concrete real-world examples of suggested activity forms.
- Adds a note-level Draft Goals link for appended Suggested Goals sections.
- Reduces extra blank lines in appended analysis markdown.
- Adds reader-level switching in AI chat responses.
- Adds a permanent safety and interpretation disclaimer in settings.
- Updates README with safety guidance, author memory explanation, ZPD/toggle guidance, a maker-note section, and the complete 64-frame analysis catalogue.

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
- git diff --check
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.6

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Beta update for BRAT testing.

Fixes:
- Supplies current local date, local time, and detected user timezone to the goal synthesis prompt.
- Requires AI-generated goal targetDate values to be today or future dates in the user's timezone.
- Drops invalid or past goal target dates during parsing instead of saving stale dates.

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
- git diff --check
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.5

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Beta update for BRAT testing.

Fixes:
- Adds durable analysis status blocks to saved journal notes so long analyses no longer appear to vanish.
- Writes progress updates into the note while analysis runs.
- Writes an analysis failure status into the note if generation cannot complete.
- Uses a compact analysis brief for long journal entries before running many analytic frames.
- Splits large perspective groups into smaller batches of four frames to reduce JSON/output failures.
- Continues with partial results if one batch fails, and records analysis notes/warnings.

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
- git diff --check
releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.4

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
Beta update for BRAT testing.

Changes:
- Adds Topological Analysis and Idiotextual Analysis.
- Deepens individual analyses to teach each frame, explain key terms, show implications, critique, predictions, further steps, and readings.
- Splits generation by analysis group with fallback generation for omitted individual frames.
- Adds per-perspective further reading lists.
- Adds ZPD reader-level setting for analysis tone and explanation depth.
- Adds group-level enable/disable controls for perspective settings.
- Limits generated goals to three synthesized next steps and adds delete buttons for proposed goals.
- Strengthens Aristotle analysis around Rhetoric, demonstration, causes, material conditions, and practical judgment.

Validation:
- npm run build
- npm run lint
- git diff --check

- Adds new philosophy, critical theory, psychoanalytic, gender/sexuality, race/decolonial, media/frame, ecology/food, and strategy/method perspectives.

- git diff --check

releases.author: MichelleGDyason

releases.name: 1.0.2-beta.1

releases.author: MichelleGDyason
releases.description:
## The Deleometer v1.0.0

Initial public release of The Deleometer for Obsidian.

### Features

- AI-powered journal entry analysis
- Multiple analysis perspectives, including:
  - psychoanalytic
  - philosophical
  - feminist
  - queer theory
  - LGBTQ+ studies
  - existential
  - Buddhist psychology
  - and more
- Perspective-specific AI chat from analysis sections
- Save chat back into the original journal analysis note
- AI-generated goal drafting from analysis
- Goal tracking dashboard
- Similar-goal consolidation workflow
- Goal syncing to the Full Calendar plugin
- Goal note frontmatter repair command
- Personality assessment and dashboard support
- Copyable AI chat messages
- Persistent OpenAI API key in plugin settings

### Notes

- Requires an OpenAI API key
- Full Calendar integration is optional
- Licensed under AGPL-3.0-only

### Installation

Download and place these files in your vault’s plugin folder:

- `main.js`
- `manifest.json`
- `styles.css`

Then enable `The Deleometer` in Obsidian Community Plugins.
releases.name: v1.0.0
